words.definition: to begin (formal); informal = start
example_sentences: The ceremony will commence at 10am precisely.
We shall commence proceedings after lunch.
The lecture commenced exactly on time.
words.pronunciation_tip: kuh-MENSS — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'commence'. Informal: 'start'. Don't use 'commence' in casual conversation.

words.definition: to ask for information (formal); informal = ask
example_sentences: I'm writing to inquire about the vacancy.
She inquired whether the position was still available.
Please inquire at the reception desk.
words.pronunciation_tip: in-KWYR — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'inquire' (British: enquire). Informal: 'ask'. Don't use 'inquire' in casual speech.

words.definition: to end or stop something (formal); informal = end, stop, sack
example_sentences: The contract will be terminated on 31st December.
His employment was terminated immediately.
They terminated the agreement without warning.
words.pronunciation_tip: TUR-mih-nayt — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'terminate'. Informal: 'end' or 'stop'. 'Terminate' in employment means 'fire'.

words.definition: in addition to what has been said (formal); informal = also, and
example_sentences: Furthermore, the report highlights several key risks.
The price is too high; furthermore, the quality is poor.
Furthermore, we recommend immediate action.
words.pronunciation_tip: FUR-ther-mawr — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'furthermore'. Informal: 'also' or 'and'. Don't use 'furthermore' in casual conversation.
words.collocations: ['furthermore, it should be noted', 'furthermore, the evidence', 'moreover/furthermore']

words.definition: to ask for something formally; informal = ask for
example_sentences: I would like to request a meeting at your earliest convenience.
She formally requested an extension on the deadline.
We request that all guests arrive by 6pm.
words.pronunciation_tip: rih-KWEST — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'request'. Informal: 'ask for'. 'Request' implies formality and respect.

words.definition: to get something (formal); informal = get
example_sentences: She obtained her degree in 2018.
The information can be obtained from the website.
Please obtain written permission before proceeding.
words.pronunciation_tip: ub-TAYN — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'obtain'. Informal: 'get'. Use 'obtain' in official and professional writing.

words.definition: before (formal); informal = before
example_sentences: Please complete the form prior to your appointment.
Prior to the meeting, she sent a detailed agenda.
All documents must be submitted prior to the deadline.
words.pronunciation_tip: PRY-er to.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'prior to'. Informal: 'before'. Use 'prior to' in official communications only.
words.collocations: ['prior to the meeting', 'prior to submission', 'prior to arrival']

words.definition: to help (formal); informal = help
example_sentences: Our staff will assist you with any queries.
She was assisted by a team of specialists.
Please do not hesitate to contact us if we can assist.
words.pronunciation_tip: uh-SIST — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'assist'. Informal: 'help'. Both are correct but 'assist' sounds more professional.

words.definition: despite this, however (formal); informal = but, still
example_sentences: The results were disappointing. Nevertheless, progress has been made.
It was a difficult decision; nevertheless, we proceeded.
Nevertheless, the project was completed on time.
words.pronunciation_tip: nev-er-thuh-LESS — four syllables, stress on last.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'nevertheless'. Informal: 'but' or 'still'. Don't use 'nevertheless' in casual speech.

words.definition: to try hard (formal); informal = try
example_sentences: We will endeavour to respond within 24 hours.
She endeavoured to complete the project on time.
Every endeavour was made to ensure accuracy.
words.pronunciation_tip: en-DEV-er — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'endeavour'. Informal: 'try'. American spelling: 'endeavor'.

words.definition: about (formal); informal = about
example_sentences: I am writing regarding your recent application.
She had several questions regarding the terms.
Please contact us regarding any outstanding issues.
words.pronunciation_tip: rih-GAR-ding — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'regarding'. Informal: 'about'. Use 'regarding' at the start of formal emails.

words.definition: large in amount or importance (formal); informal = big, large
example_sentences: There has been a substantial improvement in results.
A substantial number of complaints were received.
The changes have had a substantial impact.
words.pronunciation_tip: sub-STAN-shul — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'substantial'. Informal: 'big' or 'large'. Use in reports and formal writing.

words.definition: to put something right (formal); informal = fix, sort out
example_sentences: We will rectify the error as soon as possible.
The situation must be rectified urgently.
Steps were taken to rectify the problem.
words.pronunciation_tip: REK-tih-fy — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'rectify'. Informal: 'fix' or 'sort out'. Use in professional complaint responses.
words.collocations: ['rectify the error', 'rectify the situation', 'steps to rectify']

words.definition: instead of (formal); informal = instead of
example_sentences: She received payment in lieu of notice.
Extra holiday was offered in lieu of a pay rise.
In lieu of an apology, he sent flowers.
words.pronunciation_tip: in LYOO of.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'in lieu of'. Informal: 'instead of'. Common in employment and legal contexts.
words.collocations: ['in lieu of notice', 'payment in lieu', 'in lieu of an apology']

words.definition: to speed up a process (formal); informal = speed up
example_sentences: We will expedite your order due to the delay.
Could you expedite the review process?
She asked for the application to be expedited.
words.pronunciation_tip: EK-spih-dyt — three syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'expedite'. Informal: 'speed up'. Common in business correspondence.

words.definition: by this statement or action (very formal/legal); informal = not used
example_sentences: I hereby declare the meeting open.
You are hereby notified of the change.
I hereby certify that the above is correct.
words.pronunciation_tip: heer-BY — two syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Only used in legal and very formal documents. Never in casual speech.

words.definition: following or acting according to (legal/formal); informal = in line with, following
example_sentences: Pursuant to our agreement, payment is due on the 1st.
The decision was made pursuant to company policy.
Pursuant to your request, I have enclosed the documents.
words.pronunciation_tip: pur-SYOO-unt to — three syllables, stress on second.
words.common_mistake: Very formal/legal only. In casual writing use 'following' or 'in accordance with'.
words.collocations: ['pursuant to our agreement', 'pursuant to the policy', 'pursuant to your request']

words.definition: to find out for certain (formal); informal = find out, check
example_sentences: We need to ascertain the facts before proceeding.
She ascertained that the information was correct.
Please ascertain whether they are available.
words.pronunciation_tip: as-er-TAYN — three syllables, stress on third.
words.common_mistake: Formal: 'ascertain'. Informal: 'find out'. Common in investigative and legal writing.

words.definition: immediately (very formal/legal); informal = immediately, right away
example_sentences: The contract must be terminated forthwith.
The employee was dismissed forthwith.
Payment is required forthwith.
words.pronunciation_tip: FORTH-with — two syllables, stress on first.
words.common_mistake: Very formal, mainly legal. In everyday formal writing use 'immediately'. Never in casual speech.

words.definition: despite (very formal); informal = despite, even though
example_sentences: Notwithstanding the difficulties, the project succeeded.
Notwithstanding his qualifications, he was rejected.
The decision stands notwithstanding the appeal.
words.pronunciation_tip: not-with-STAN-ding — four syllables, stress on third.
words.common_mistake: Very formal/legal. In normal formal writing use 'despite'. Never in casual speech.
